title: Churdhar Sanctuary
tagline: Churdhar Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Himachal Pradesh

overview.short: Churdhar Sanctuary is located in the Sirmaur district of the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh.
overview.description: Churdhar Sanctuary is located in the Sirmaur district of the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. The total covered area of this sanctuary is 56.16 square kilometers, as notified on 15 November 1985. The fauna includes Himalayan black bear, Barking deer, Musk deer, Langur and Leopards. The work of administration at Churdhar is taken care by Chureshwar Sewa Samiti.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Churdhar Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Churdhar Sanctuary is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Churdhar Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Shimla Airport (Jubbarhatti) (39 km) and the nearest railway station is Chandigarh Railway Station (60 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Churdhar Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Churdhar Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Churdhar Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Churdhar Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.
